description: A stricter constraint was added before existing data was cleaned or application errors were mapped.
body: Failure mode: migration fails on existing nulls, duplicates, or orphan records, or live writes start throwing raw database errors. Diagnostic trigger: schema diff adds not-null, unique, check, or foreign key constraints with no preflight query or cleanup plan. Do-not-use signal: do not treat a constraint as safe merely because it encodes the desired invariant. Recovery: query for violations, clean or backfill data, add compatible application handling for violations, and validate the constraint in a staged migration if supported. Risk boundary: uniqueness and foreign keys may reveal hidden concurrency or delete semantics; verify affected write and delete paths.
